reasoning:
The objective to "terminate the Public Service Loan Forgiveness program" is unlikely to be marked as "complete" on the Project 2025 Tracker before July 1, 2025. Given that PSLF was established by Congress (in 2007), it cannot be completely terminated by executive action alone. While the Trump administration has issued executive orders limiting the program (reclassifying "public service" to exclude certain nonprofits) and initiated a rulemaking process, these actions only restrict the program rather than terminate it, justifying its current "in progress" status. 

To formally terminate PSLF, Congress would need to pass legislation, most likely as part of the upcoming budget reconciliation package. Although Congress passed the budget resolution in April 2025 to unlock reconciliation, drafting, CBO-scoring, negotiating, and passing a massive reconciliation bill (expected to cover expansive tax cuts and spending cuts) typically takes many months. Achieving this within the short remaining window (between mid-May and July 1, 2025) is highly improbable, as such bills usually face delays over intra-party negotiations and Senate procedural hurdles (the Byrd Rule) stretching into the fall or winter. Even if the bill were passed by the deadline, it is not certain that Republicans would fully terminate PSLF instead of heavily reforming or grandfathering it. Thus, the spreadsheet will overwhelmingly likely still categorize the objective as either "in progress" or similar by the resolution date.
